description: Keep dynamo as the 1866 self-excited industrial machine stage and preserve electric_generators_dynamos as the distinct 1831 Faraday disk predecessor.

checks.reason: Siemens Historical Institute dates the practical dynamo machine to 1866, distinct from the Faraday disk.
The separate Faraday Disk Generator must retain its experimental 1831 chronology.
The self-excited machine is a later generator stage, not another label for Faraday's disk.
Insulated field and armature windings are directly documented components.
The broad electricity field must not replace the precise Faraday generator lineage.
No one substitutable prime mover is intrinsic to self-excitation.
Classical Monumental Construction is outside the electrical-machine boundary.
The direct historical edge must point to the concrete Faraday generator, which already carries the earlier electromagnetism lineage.
The 1831 Faraday node must never depend on or be reverse-merged with the later 1866 self-excited dynamo.
Utility-station deployment is downstream and must not redefine the machine milestone.
